name: Motion & Interaction System
description: Standardized motion rules for all animations, transitions, and micro-interactions. Every motion must be purposeful.
principles: Motion communicates hierarchy and state changes — never decoration alone.
Fast by default. Users should never wait for an animation to complete before acting.
Respect prefers-reduced-motion. All motion must be gracefully degradable.
Use CSS transitions for simple state changes. Reserve JS animation for orchestrated sequences.

instant.usage: Immediate response. Color changes, opacity toggles with no motion.

fast.usage: Micro-interactions: button press, checkbox toggle, ripple, hover state changes.

normal.usage: Standard transitions: dropdown open, panel slide, tab switch.

slow.usage: Complex reveals: modal entry, page section fade-in, accordion expand.

slower.usage: Full page transitions, hero animations, onboarding sequences.

default.usage: General purpose. Most transitions use this.

in.usage: Elements leaving the screen. Accelerates out of view.

out.usage: Elements entering the screen. Decelerates into position.

inOut.usage: Elements moving across screen. Smooth start and end.

bounce.usage: Playful feedback. Use sparingly: toggle switches, success states.

transitions.description: Pre-defined transition patterns for common interactions

fadeIn.usage: Content appearing: toasts, tooltips, lazy-loaded images

fadeOut.usage: Content disappearing: dismissed toasts, closing tooltips

slideUp.usage: Modals, bottom sheets, dropdown menus appearing

toggleSwitch.effect: Thumb slides to opposite end of track

checkboxCheck.effect: Checkmark draws in via stroke-dashoffset animation

ripple.effect: Radial circle expands from click point, fades out
ripple.usage: Optional — use for tactile feedback on touch devices

shimmer.effect: Gradient sweeps left to right across skeleton elements

focusRing.note: Use :focus-visible, not :focus, to avoid showing on mouse click

pageTransitions.rule: Use when navigating between views in SPAs. Keep under 400ms.

reducedMotion.rule: Replace all animations with instant state changes. No transform animations. Opacity transitions allowed but must be <= 100ms.
reducedMotion.css: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }
